# NEXUS AI Receptionist

> NEXUS is an AI-powered phone receptionist for small businesses. It answers every call 24/7, books appointments via Google Calendar, takes messages, qualifies leads, and sends instant call summaries — starting at $25/month. No contracts. Setup in 24–48 hours.

## Product Overview

- [Homepage](https://nexus-saas-receptionist.vercel.app/): Full product overview, features, pricing, and live demo
- Answers calls in 1 ring, 24/7 including nights, weekends, and holidays
- Handles unlimited simultaneous calls — no busy signals, no hold times
- Books appointments directly into Google Calendar
- Sends call transcripts and summaries via email and SMS
- Integrates with 7,000+ apps via Zapier (Pro and above)
- Custom AI agent name, voice, and personality per business

## Pricing

- **Lite** — $25/month: 30 calls/month, 24/7 answering, Google Calendar booking, email summaries
- **Pro** — $149/month: 500 minutes/month, SMS + email confirmations, Slack alerts, smart call forwarding, Zapier integration
- **Scale** — $349/month: 1,500 minutes/month, multi-destination routing, Salesforce and HubSpot integrations, 90-day call recordings
- **Enterprise** — Custom pricing: unlimited minutes, dedicated support, SLA guarantee

## Comparisons

- [NEXUS vs Ruby Receptionists](https://nexus-saas-receptionist.vercel.app/blog/nexus-vs-ruby-receptionists): NEXUS starts at $25/mo vs Ruby's $235/mo entry point — 94% cheaper at the entry tier
- [NEXUS vs Smith.ai](https://nexus-saas-receptionist.vercel.app/blog/nexus-vs-smith-ai): NEXUS Pro handles 165+ calls/mo for $149 vs Smith.ai at ~$975/mo for 100 calls — saving $9,900+/year
- [NEXUS vs AnswerConnect](https://nexus-saas-receptionist.vercel.app/blog/nexus-vs-answerconnect): Flat-rate vs per-minute pricing comparison
- [NEXUS vs Davinci Virtual](https://nexus-saas-receptionist.vercel.app/blog/nexus-vs-davinci-virtual): 24/7 AI coverage vs business-hours-only live agents
- [NEXUS vs PATLive](https://nexus-saas-receptionist.vercel.app/blog/nexus-vs-patlive): Flat-rate predictable billing vs per-minute variable cost
- [NEXUS vs Abby Connect](https://nexus-saas-receptionist.vercel.app/blog/nexus-vs-abby-connect): Unlimited simultaneous calls vs capped concurrent agent plans

## Target Industries

- Plumbers, HVAC, electricians, and home service trades
- Medical offices, dental practices, and healthcare providers
- Property management companies and real estate agencies
- Law firms and professional services
- Fitness studios, salons, and wellness businesses
- Restaurants and hospitality

## Key Facts

- Average business misses 62% of inbound calls
- Human receptionist costs ~$48,000/year; NEXUS starts at $300/year
- Setup time: 24–48 hours from signup to live
- Call forwarding setup: ~5 minutes with any phone provider
- No contracts — cancel anytime

## Blog

- [Blog index](https://nexus-saas-receptionist.vercel.app/blog): Competitor comparisons and guides for AI receptionist buyers
